title: Passing a string with a space to `--network-alias` creates a container unable to resolve names (DNS)
body:
### Issue Description

Creating a container with `--network-alias`, where the alias contains a space, will prevent the new container from resolving domain names.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman network create test`
2. `podman container run --rm -it --network test --network-alias "a b" alpine`
3. Inside the container: `ping podman.io`

Also reproducible if the container is created through the socket API (`podman system service`).


### Describe the results you received

```
/ # ping podman.io
ping: bad address 'podman.io'
```

### Describe the results you expected

Either:
* If spaces are valid in network aliases, name resolution should work as expected.
* If spaces are not valid in network aliases, an error should be given at the moment of container creation.

comments.body: Seems likely we're crashing aardvark-dns by passing impossible domain names into it. Podman should catch aliases with invalid characters not allowed in domain names - like spaces - and error instead of proceeding.
Podman should catch it AND aardvark-dns should reject it with an error.
https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/issues/591 for the aardvark side, though aardvark is called by netavark, so I *think* the correction should probably go there instead.

ref https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/1019

I don't think we can reject these names without causing compatibility problems. Of course the names are never valid but I think we should ignore them instead or a hard error.
